samples.gvd:
module asyn_fifo(
    input wclk,
    input rclk,
    input wrstn,
    input rrstn,
    input winc,
    input rinc,
    input wdata
);
dmodule
module dual_port_RAM (
  input               wclk,
  input               wenc,
  input      [3:0]    waddr,
  input      [7:0]    wdata,
  input               rclk,
  input               renc,
  input      [3:0]    raddr,
  output reg [7:0]    rdata
);
endmodule
samples.xml_description:
<module id="asyn_fifo">
    <description>Implement an asynchronous FIFO, FIFO bit width and depth can be configured(parameter DEPTH = 16, parameter WIDTH = 8).</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="wclk">Write clock.</input>
        <input id="rclk">Read clock.</input>
        <input id="rstn">Active-low reset.</input>
        <input id="winc">Write increment input.</input>
        <input id="rinc">Read increment input.</input>
        <input id="wdata" width_description="[WIDTH-1:0]">Write data input.</input>
        <output id="wfull">Write full output.</output>
        <output id="rempty">Read empty output.</output>
        <output id="rdata" width_description="[WIDTH-1:0]">Read data output.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <param id="WIDTH">Data width of FIFO.</param>
        <param id="DEPTH">Depth of FIFO.</param>
    </parameter_description>
    <implementation>
    <para>1. Instantiate dual-port RAM as a submodule.</para>
    <para>2. Use wclk, wenc, waddr, wdata, rclk, renc, raddr, rdata.</para>
    <para>3. Use separate clock for read and write operations.</para>
    <para>4. Use Gray code for read and write pointers.</para>
    <para>5. Use buffer registers to store previous read and write pointers.</para>
    <para>6. Use combinational logic to determine full and empty conditions.</para>
    <para>7. Use write increment and read increment inputs.</para>
    </implementation>
</module>
